message.content: Adversaries may exploit the setuid and setgid bits on Linux and macOS to execute code with elevated privileges. These bits, when set on an application binary, allow the application to run with the privileges of the owning user or group, rather than the current user. This can be leveraged by attackers to maintain elevated access without needing root permissions to modify the sudoers file. Common exploitation methods include setting these bits on their own malware or targeting existing binaries with these bits already set. This technique is often used in "shell escape" scenarios to bypass restricted execution environments. Mitigation strategies include regularly auditing file permissions, using the `find` command to identify and review files with setuid/setgid bits, and minimizing the use of these bits to only essential applications.
